w1.title: The Lost Traveler
w1.story: You cross the golden desert when you spot an old man struggling with heavy bags. The caravan is moving fast...

choices.text: Stop and help him carry his bags
choices.outcome: You helped the traveler and he blessed you. The Prophet said: The best of people are those most beneficial to others.

choices.text: Keep walking — the caravan cannot wait
choices.outcome: You kept moving but felt uneasy. Sometimes slowing down is the right choice.

w1.title: The Merchant Honesty
w1.story: A merchant gives you extra change by mistake. You realize only after walking away. You have 10 extra coins...

choices.text: Return to the merchant and give back the coins
choices.outcome: SubhanAllah! Your honesty made the merchant cry with joy. Be truthful for truth leads to righteousness.

choices.text: Keep the coins — it was his mistake
choices.outcome: The coins felt heavy in your pocket. Something did not feel right inside.

w1.title: The Thirsty Camel
w1.story: Your camel is thirsty and there is water ahead, but a stray dog whimpers nearby with no one to care for it...

choices.text: Share some water with the dog too
choices.outcome: A woman was once forgiven all her sins for giving water to a thirsty dog. You earned great reward!

choices.text: Use all water for your camel only
choices.outcome: Your camel was satisfied, but you passed a chance to earn great reward.

w1.title: Patience at Dawn
w1.story: Fajr time arrives. You are exhausted from the long desert journey. Your blanket is warm and the sand is cold...

choices.text: Wake up, make wudu, and pray Fajr
choices.outcome: Allahu Akbar! Two rakats at Fajr are better than the world and all it contains. Your heart feels light.

choices.text: Sleep a little more and pray later
choices.outcome: You missed the blessing of Fajr time. Tomorrow try harder. Every dawn is a new chance.

w2.title: The Neighbor Need
w2.story: Your neighbor knocks. Her family has not eaten today. You have just enough food for your family...

choices.text: Share half your food with her family
choices.outcome: He is not a believer who eats his fill while his neighbor goes hungry. You chose the way of the Prophet.

choices.text: Say you do not have enough and close the door
choices.outcome: That night your meal did not taste as good as it should. Generosity multiplies blessings.

w2.title: The Angry Friend
w2.story: Your best friend broke your favourite toy by accident. You feel very angry. What do you do?

choices.text: Take a deep breath and forgive him
choices.outcome: The Prophet said: The strong person is not the one who can wrestle but the one who controls themselves when angry.

choices.text: Yell at him and walk away
choices.outcome: Your friend cried. Anger rarely solves things and often breaks what we love most.
